title: Dendrorkis paulensis

Dendropanax morototoni, also known as Dendropanax paulensis, is a plant species belonging to the Araliaceae family, commonly known as a shrub or small tree native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiparasitic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Dendropanax morototoni include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, pain, and digestive problems.
